data.title: KNT Holdings Extends Placing Period and Revises Rights Issue Timetable
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from KNT Holdings Limited ( (HK:1025) ) is now available.

KNT Holdings Limited has entered into a supplemental agreement with its placing agent to extend the timetable for a previously announced rights issue and associated placing of unsubscribed shares. The changes give the placing agent more time to complete administrative work and do not alter the core commercial terms of the original placing arrangement.

Under the revised schedule, the placing of unsubscribed rights shares and non-qualifying shareholders’ unsold rights shares will now run from 9 February 2026 to 4:00 p.m. on 11 March 2026. The rights issue and placing are now expected to become unconditional by 4:00 p.m. on 12 March 2026, which is also the revised settlement and completion date, slightly delaying capital-raising but providing greater execution certainty for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1025) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.27 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KNT Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1025 Stock Forecast page.

More about KNT Holdings Limited

KNT Holdings Limited is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 1025. The group operates in Hong Kong’s capital markets as a listed issuer and accesses equity financing tools such as rights issues and share placings to support its corporate and financial needs.

Average Trading Volume: 1,870,262

An announcement from Fibocom Wireless, Inc. Class H ( (HK:0638) ) is now available.

Fibocom Wireless Inc. announced that shareholders approved all resolutions at its first extraordinary general meeting of 2026, including the adoption and administration of the 2026 Employee Stock Ownership Plan and authorization for the board to handle related matters. The meeting, held in Shenzhen with about 37% of voting shares represented, saw interested insiders and certain shareholders abstain from voting on the plan in line with listing rules, reinforcing compliance standards and enabling the company to proceed with employee incentive measures.

The EGM was validly convened under PRC company law and the company’s articles, with no resolutions rejected, amended or newly proposed during the session. Broad board attendance, aside from one director absent for work reasons, alongside detailed disclosure of voting rights and treasury share treatment, underscores Fibocom’s emphasis on transparent governance as it implements the new stock ownership scheme for staff.

More about Fibocom Wireless, Inc. Class H

Fibocom Wireless Inc. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong, engaging in the wireless communications sector. The company issues both A shares and H shares, with a significant base of domestic and international shareholders participating in its corporate governance.

data.title: Muyuan Foods’ February Hog Revenue Drops Nearly 24% on Lower Prices
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Muyuan Foods Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2714) ) is now available.

Muyuan Foods reported that in February 2026 it sold about 4.60 million finished hogs, a slight year-on-year volume decline of 0.77%, with roughly 2.40 million heads sold to its wholly owned meat subsidiary. The average selling price fell 18.72% to RMB11.59 per kilogram, driving finished hog sales revenue down 23.98% year on year to about RMB6.41 billion and 2026 year-to-date hog revenue to RMB16.97 billion.

The company cautioned that sharp swings in hog prices remain a key systemic risk for the entire sector and could materially affect its profitability, while animal diseases continue to pose significant operational threats. Management emphasized that the disclosed sales data are unaudited and may differ from figures in forthcoming periodic reports, urging shareholders and potential investors to pay close attention to these risks when trading the company’s securities.

More about Muyuan Foods Co Ltd Class H

Muyuan Foods Co., Ltd. is a People’s Republic of China-based hog producer listed in Hong Kong, focusing on large-scale hog breeding and sales. The company also sells a significant portion of its finished hogs to its wholly owned subsidiary Muyuan Meat Co., Ltd., integrating upstream livestock production with downstream meat processing and distribution.

Average Trading Volume: 5,531,793

Synertone Communication Corporation ( (HK:1613) ) has shared an update.

Synertone Communication Corporation has entered into a placing agreement to issue up to 1 billion new shares at HK$0.108 each through Arta Asset Management, representing about 186.61% of its existing share capital and 65.11% of the enlarged capital if fully placed. The deal, to be executed under a specific mandate and subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting and other conditions, could raise net proceeds of approximately HK$106.6 million.

The company plans to use about 83.8% of the funds, or roughly HK$89.3 million, to expand its Building Intelligence Business, with the remaining 16.2% earmarked for working capital and general corporate purposes. The proposed placing, which requires stock exchange approval for listing of the new shares, would significantly dilute existing shareholders but is positioned as a key funding step to support Synertone’s strategic push into building intelligence solutions and to strengthen its balance sheet.

An update from Zai Lab Ltd ( (HK:9688) ) is now available.

Zai Lab has granted new equity awards under its 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, issuing 8,000 share options representing 80,000 underlying shares to four employee participants and 12,344 restricted share units representing 123,440 underlying shares to nine employees. The options, priced at US$19.48 per ADS, vest over four years without performance targets or clawback provisions, a structure the board’s compensation committee says is consistent with market practice and designed to retain talent, potentially leading to incremental dilution but aligning employee interests with long-term shareholder value.

The share options carry a ten-year validity period and will lapse upon expiry or earlier termination of employment, reinforcing retention incentives over time. The restricted share units are granted at no purchase price, using prevailing NASDAQ and Hong Kong share prices as reference points, underscoring Zai Lab’s ongoing use of equity-based compensation to strengthen its competitive position in recruiting and retaining key staff in the biopharmaceutical sector.

data.title: Edensoft Sets Formal Governance Framework for Nomination Committee
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Edensoft Holdings Limited ( (HK:1147) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Edensoft Holdings Limited has formalized the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, aligning its governance practices with the Corporate Governance Code under the Hong Kong Listing Rules. The committee must comprise at least three members, a majority of whom are independent non-executive directors, and include at least one director of a different gender, with meetings held at least annually and governed by clear quorum, voting and procedural rules.

The Board will appoint the Nomination Committee members and its chairman, who will normally be either the Board chairman or an independent non-executive director, while ensuring the Board chairman does not chair discussions on his own succession. The rules also specify that only committee members may vote, although other directors, executives or external advisers may be invited to attend, and resolutions can be passed in person, via electronic means or in writing, reinforcing procedural clarity and transparency in board appointments and succession planning.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1147) stock is a Buy with a HK$0.18 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Edensoft Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1147 Stock Forecast page.

More about Edensoft Holdings Limited

Edensoft Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating under the corporate governance framework required by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The firm maintains a board structure that includes independent non-executive directors and formal board committees to oversee key governance functions and regulatory compliance.

Average Trading Volume: 4,305,000

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

The latest update is out from Viva China Holdings Limited ( (HK:0933) ).

Viva Goods Company Limited expects a sharp turnaround for the year ending 31 December 2025, projecting at least HK$150 million in profit attributable to equity holders versus a loss a year earlier. data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from REF Holdings Limited ( (HK:1631) ).

REF Holdings Limited plans to overhaul its constitutional documents, proposing amendments to its existing memorandum and articles of association to align with updated Hong Kong Listing Rules and Cayman Islands laws. The revisions focus on enabling hybrid shareholder meetings, electronic voting and electronic dissemination of corporate communications, as well as accommodating the holding of treasury shares under the latest regulatory regime.

The board intends to adopt a fourth amended and restated memorandum and articles of association, subject to shareholder approval by special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 6 May 2026. A circular detailing the proposed changes and convening the meeting will be sent to shareholders, signaling a move to modernize corporate governance practices and increase operational flexibility in capital management and shareholder engagement.

REF Holdings Limited ( (HK:1631) ) has issued an announcement.

REF Holdings Limited reported a decline in revenue to HK$92.3 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, down from HK$106.4 million a year earlier, with gross profit falling to HK$46.6 million. Net profit attributable to shareholders nearly halved to HK$3.0 million, while earnings per share slipped to 1.16 HK cents, reflecting pressure on margins despite lower administrative and finance costs.

The group modestly improved credit quality, recording a small reversal of expected credit loss allowances on trade receivables, and benefited from slightly higher other gains. However, reduced profitability and earnings underscore a more challenging operating environment and may raise concerns among investors about growth momentum and the sustainability of returns going into future periods.

data.title: Prada Lifts 2025 Revenues as Miu Miu and Versace Bolster Global Luxury Push
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Prada SpA ( (HK:1913) ) has issued an update.

Prada reported consolidated net revenues of €5.72 billion for 2025, up 9.1% at constant exchange rates, driven by 9.3% retail sales growth and strong momentum at Miu Miu, which surged 34.8%. Sales rose across all major regions, with particularly robust gains in the Americas and the Middle East, underscoring broad-based consumer demand for the group’s luxury brands.

Profitability remained solid despite significant investment and currency headwinds, with adjusted EBIT of €1.32 billion and a 23.2% margin, while group profit edged up 1.6% to €851.9 million. The late-2025 consolidation of Versace was dilutive to margins and contributed to a net financial deficit of €465.8 million, but it strategically enhances Prada’s brand portfolio, and the board proposed a cash dividend of €0.166 per share, signaling confidence in ongoing earnings strength.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1913) stock is a Buy with a HK$45.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Prada SpA stock, see the HK:1913 Stock Forecast page.

More about Prada SpA

Prada S.p.A., listed in Hong Kong, is a global leader in the luxury industry with brands including Prada, Miu Miu, Versace, Church’s, Car Shoe, Pasticceria Marchesi 1824 and Luna Rossa. The group designs and produces ready-to-wear, leather goods, footwear and jewellery, and distributes in over 70 countries through 843 directly operated stores, e-commerce, selected e-tailers and department stores, while also operating in eyewear and beauty via licensing agreements.

With 25 owned factories mainly in Italy and around 18,000 employees, Prada emphasizes creativity and sustainable growth across its portfolio of high-end brands. The recent addition of Versace, acquired in December 2025, further strengthens its position in Italian luxury fashion and expands its global footprint in the premium segment.

China Huajun Group Limited ( (HK:0377) ) has provided an announcement.

China Huajun Group Limited has disclosed that its current public float stands at about 15.68%, below the minimum level required by Hong Kong listing rules, and remains in breach despite previous updates. The company has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in its shares while it works to restore compliance.

The group expects completion of its debt restructuring scheme, including adjudication of creditor claims and issuance of Scheme Shares, around June 2026, which will significantly alter its shareholding structure and boost the public float. Management has committed to providing monthly updates on progress, signaling ongoing regulatory scrutiny and a transitional period for existing shareholders as new creditor and subscriber stakes dilute the controlling shareholder’s position.

Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited ( (HK:1406) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited has appointed Grant Thornton Advisory Services Limited as forensic advisor to its Separate Special Committee to conduct an independent investigation into alleged material misstatements in its listing prospectus. The move is part of resumption guidance requirements from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, under which the company must assess the impact of the allegations on its business and finances, disclose key findings, and implement remedial actions before trading in its shares, suspended since April 2025, can potentially resume.

The forensic investigation is expected to influence the company’s path to regulatory compliance and trading resumption, with Grant Thornton reporting its conclusions to both the committee and the board. Continued suspension of trading underscores the seriousness of the allegations, and shareholders and potential investors have been urged to exercise caution while they await further announcements on the investigation’s outcomes and any resulting corporate or governance measures.

More about Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited

Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited is a Hong Kong-listed healthcare company focused on providing medical services, operating within the broader medical and clinical services industry. The group serves patients in Hong Kong and potentially wider regional markets, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1406 until their current suspension.

data.title: Winson Holdings refreshes board with new independent director
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Winson Holdings Hong Kong Ltd ( (HK:6812) ) has shared an announcement.

Winson Holdings Hong Kong Ltd has announced board changes effective 16 March 2026, with independent non-executive director Chan Chun Sing resigning to focus on other business engagements and stepping down from both the audit and nomination committees. The board noted Chan has no disagreement with the company and expressed appreciation for his contributions, indicating a smooth and non-contentious transition.

The company has appointed seasoned banker Ng Moon Fai as an independent non-executive director and member of the audit and nomination committees, also effective 16 March 2026. Ng brings nearly three decades of banking and regional management experience across Hong Kong, the Greater Bay Area and ASEAN, and his appointment, on a two-year term with an annual fee of HK$240,000, is expected to strengthen the group’s governance and financial oversight capabilities.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6812) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.31 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Winson Holdings Hong Kong Ltd stock, see the HK:6812 Stock Forecast page.

More about Winson Holdings Hong Kong Ltd

Winson Holdings Hong Kong Ltd, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through a group structure focused on its Hong Kong-based businesses. The company is overseen by a board that includes executive and independent non-executive directors, supported by key governance committees such as the audit and nomination committees.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Qfin Holdings ( (HK:3660) ).

Qfin Holdings has scheduled a board meeting in Hong Kong on 17 March 2026 to approve its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended 31 December 2025, and to consider any potential dividend recommendation. The company plans to publish these 2025 results on 18 March 2026 before market trading begins in Hong Kong, giving investors timely visibility into its latest financial performance.

Management will also host an earnings conference call on 17 March 2026 U.S. Eastern Time, with a simultaneous morning session on 18 March 2026 in Beijing and Hong Kong. A live webcast and an archived replay will be made available via the company’s investor relations website, underscoring its emphasis on transparency and accessibility for global shareholders and analysts.
